title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the weight needed to stretch the wire, we need to use the formula for Young's modulus, which relates stress and strain in a material.

title: Young's Modulus Formula
text: The formula for Young's modulus (Y) is given by: $$Y=\frac{\frac{F}{A}}{\frac{\Delta L}{L}}$$, where F is the force applied, A is the cross-sectional area, ΔL is the change in length, and L is the original length.

title: Calculate the Cross-Sectional Area
text: The cross-sectional area (A) of the wire can be calculated using the formula for the area of a circle: $$A=\pi \left(\frac{d}{2}\right)^2$$, where $$d$$ is the diameter of the wire.

title: Calculate the Cross-Sectional Area
text: Given the diameter of the wire is $$1.0$$ mm, calculate the cross-sectional area.

title: Rearrange the Young's Modulus Formula
text: Rearrange the formula to solve for the force (F): $$F=\frac{Y A \Delta L}{L}$$.

title: Calculate the Force
text: Using the rearranged formula $$F=\frac{Y A \Delta L}{L}$$, calculate the force needed to stretch the wire by $$3.0$$ mm.
